howItWorks.intro: This sump pump size calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Head = Vertical Lift + (Horizontal Run x 0.1)
Required GPH = Basement Area x 1.5 x Water Risk Multiplier x Head Loss Factor
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What size sump pump do I need?
faq.answer: For most homes: 1/3 HP handles up to 1800 GPH, sufficient for average basements. 1/2 HP (3000 GPH) for larger basements or moderate water. 3/4-1 HP for high water table areas or long discharge runs.

faq.question: Do I need a backup sump pump?
faq.answer: Yes if your area floods frequently or you have valuable items in the basement. Battery backup pumps run 5-7 hours during power outages. A water alarm ($15-25) is the minimum every homeowner should have.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What basement area should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate basement area value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Sump Pump Size Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the Sump Pump Size Calculator?
sections.body: The Sump Pump Size Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your basement area, water table risk, discharge height, you get instant results including recommended pump capacity, recommended horsepower, total dynamic head. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting recommended pump capacity right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your basement area and need to find the right recommended pump capacity. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Basement Area (sq ft)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your basement area — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: water table risk, discharge height, horizontal pipe run. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your recommended pump capacity, recommended horsepower, total dynamic head. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your basement area before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both recommended pump capacity and recommended horsepower — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your horizontal pipe run, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
